# The Flutter tooling requires that developers have CMake 3.10 or later
# installed. You should not increase this version, as doing so will cause
# the plugin to fail to compile for some customers of the plugin.
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.10)

# Project-level configuration.
set(PROJECT_NAME "native_mouse_cursor")
# Enable C as well as CXX: the Wayland protocol glue generated by wayland-scanner
# is C, and a CXX-only project silently drops .c sources from the build (which
# left the zwp_*_interface symbols undefined at link time).
project(${PROJECT_NAME} LANGUAGES CXX C)

# This value is used when generating builds using this plugin, so it must
# not be changed.
set(PLUGIN_NAME "native_mouse_cursor_plugin")

# Any new source files that you add to the plugin should be added here.
list(APPEND PLUGIN_SOURCES
  "native_mouse_cursor_plugin.cc"
  "nmc_pointer.cc"
)

# Define the plugin library target. Its name must not be changed (see comment
# on PLUGIN_NAME above).
add_library(${PLUGIN_NAME} SHARED
  ${PLUGIN_SOURCES}
)

# Apply a standard set of build settings that are configured in the
# application-level CMakeLists.txt. This can be removed for plugins that want
# full control over build settings.
apply_standard_settings(${PLUGIN_NAME})

# Symbols are hidden by default to reduce the chance of accidental conflicts
# between plugins. This should not be removed; any symbols that should be
# exported should be explicitly exported with the FLUTTER_PLUGIN_EXPORT macro.
set_target_properties(${PLUGIN_NAME} PROPERTIES
  CXX_VISIBILITY_PRESET hidden)
target_compile_definitions(${PLUGIN_NAME} PRIVATE FLUTTER_PLUGIN_IMPL)

# Source include directories and library dependencies. Add any plugin-specific
# dependencies here.
target_include_directories(${PLUGIN_NAME} INTERFACE
  "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include")
target_link_libraries(${PLUGIN_NAME} PRIVATE flutter)
target_link_libraries(${PLUGIN_NAME} PRIVATE PkgConfig::GTK)

# Pointer-warp (X11) and lock-pointer (Wayland) support are resolved at RUNTIME
# via dlsym (see the .cc) — we deliberately do NOT link libX11 or
# libwayland-client. Dragging those system libs onto the executable link line
# breaks on snap-packaged Flutter: the old bundled toolchain (Clang 10 / older
# GLIBC) can't resolve their newer GLIBC symbols (dlopen@GLIBC_2.34,
# __isoc23_strtol@2.38, …) → an undefined-reference link error in the HOST app.
#
# We only need HEADERS:
#   • gdk-x11-3.0 for gdk/gdkx.h (GDK_IS_X11_DISPLAY etc.)
#   • wayland-client + gdk-wayland-3.0 for gdk/gdkwayland.h, wl_proxy / wl_fixed_t
#     / WL_MARSHAL_FLAG_DESTROY and the protocol interface decls.
# Their symbols are resolved at runtime from libs GDK already loaded. The
# gdk-*-3.0 include dirs arrive with GTK's include paths; we add wayland-client's
# include dir when present. libdl is part of glibc. No extra link libraries — so
# an X11-only or header-less host builds fine (the matching path compiles/short-
# circuits out at runtime).
pkg_check_modules(WAYLAND_CLIENT IMPORTED_TARGET wayland-client)
if(WAYLAND_CLIENT_FOUND)
  target_include_directories(${PLUGIN_NAME} PRIVATE
    ${WAYLAND_CLIENT_INCLUDE_DIRS})

  # Generate the CORRECT wl_interface descriptor tables for the two protocols we
  # bind, from the system wayland-protocols XML, using wayland-scanner. We
  # compile ONLY the generated "*-protocol.c" (pure interface/message DATA — it
  # makes NO wl_proxy_* calls), so we still don't link libwayland-client (the
  # snap GLIBC trap). The generated client HEADER gives us the opcodes/enums and
  # the extern wl_interface decls; our .cc does the actual marshalling via the
  # dlsym'd wl_proxy_marshal_flags. If wayland-scanner or the XML is missing, we
  # skip — and the .cc's NMC_WAYLAND_HAVE_PROTOCOLS guard compiles the lock path
  # out (Wayland then falls back to a clamped drag).
  find_program(WAYLAND_SCANNER wayland-scanner)
  pkg_get_variable(WL_PROTO_DIR wayland-protocols pkgdatadir)
  set(_pc_xml "${WL_PROTO_DIR}/unstable/pointer-constraints/pointer-constraints-unstable-v1.xml")
  set(_rp_xml "${WL_PROTO_DIR}/unstable/relative-pointer/relative-pointer-unstable-v1.xml")
  if(WAYLAND_SCANNER AND WL_PROTO_DIR AND EXISTS "${_pc_xml}" AND EXISTS "${_rp_xml}")
    set(_gen "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/wl_generated")
    file(MAKE_DIRECTORY "${_gen}")
    add_custom_command(
      OUTPUT "${_gen}/pointer-constraints-unstable-v1-protocol.c"
             "${_gen}/pointer-constraints-unstable-v1-client-protocol.h"
      COMMAND ${WAYLAND_SCANNER} private-code "${_pc_xml}"
              "${_gen}/pointer-constraints-unstable-v1-protocol.c"
      COMMAND ${WAYLAND_SCANNER} client-header "${_pc_xml}"
              "${_gen}/pointer-constraints-unstable-v1-client-protocol.h"
      DEPENDS "${_pc_xml}" VERBATIM)
    add_custom_command(
      OUTPUT "${_gen}/relative-pointer-unstable-v1-protocol.c"
             "${_gen}/relative-pointer-unstable-v1-client-protocol.h"
      COMMAND ${WAYLAND_SCANNER} private-code "${_rp_xml}"
              "${_gen}/relative-pointer-unstable-v1-protocol.c"
      COMMAND ${WAYLAND_SCANNER} client-header "${_rp_xml}"
              "${_gen}/relative-pointer-unstable-v1-client-protocol.h"
      DEPENDS "${_rp_xml}" VERBATIM)
    target_sources(${PLUGIN_NAME} PRIVATE
      "${_gen}/pointer-constraints-unstable-v1-protocol.c"
      "${_gen}/relative-pointer-unstable-v1-protocol.c")
    target_include_directories(${PLUGIN_NAME} PRIVATE "${_gen}")
    target_compile_definitions(${PLUGIN_NAME} PRIVATE NMC_WAYLAND_HAVE_PROTOCOLS)

    # Pointer-warp (wp_pointer_warp_v1, staging — wayland-protocols >= 1.45) is
    # too new to require from the host's pkgdatadir, so we VENDOR its XML in
    # linux/wayland_protocols/ and scan that. Same model: compile only the
    # generated DATA table, marshal via the dlsym'd wl_proxy_marshal_flags.
    # Independent NMC_WAYLAND_HAVE_WARP guard so the build still works (lock-only)
    # if the vendored XML is removed; runtime still gates on the compositor
    # actually advertising the global.
    set(_pw_xml "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/wayland_protocols/pointer-warp-v1.xml")
    if(EXISTS "${_pw_xml}")
      add_custom_command(
        OUTPUT "${_gen}/pointer-warp-v1-protocol.c"
               "${_gen}/pointer-warp-v1-client-protocol.h"
        COMMAND ${WAYLAND_SCANNER} private-code "${_pw_xml}"
                "${_gen}/pointer-warp-v1-protocol.c"
        COMMAND ${WAYLAND_SCANNER} client-header "${_pw_xml}"
                "${_gen}/pointer-warp-v1-client-protocol.h"
        DEPENDS "${_pw_xml}" VERBATIM)
      target_sources(${PLUGIN_NAME} PRIVATE
        "${_gen}/pointer-warp-v1-protocol.c")
      target_compile_definitions(${PLUGIN_NAME} PRIVATE NMC_WAYLAND_HAVE_WARP)
    endif()
  endif()
endif()

# List of absolute paths to libraries that should be bundled with the plugin.
# This list could contain prebuilt libraries, or libraries created by an
# external build triggered from this build file.
set(native_mouse_cursor_bundled_libraries
  ""
  PARENT_SCOPE
)
